div.head: The British Ambassador (Lothian) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Sir: I have the honour, under instructions from His Majesty’s Principal Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs to inform you that a proclamation has been issued in London specifying the articles to be treated as contraband of war by His Majesty’s Government in the United Kingdom. These articles are as follows:—

list.label: (a)
list.item: All kinds of arms, ammunition, explosives, chemicals, or appliances suitable for use in chemical warfare and machines for their manufacture or repair; component parts thereof; articles necessary or convenient for their use; materials or ingredients used in their manufacture; articles necessary or convenient for the production or use of such materials or ingredients.
list.label: (b)
list.item: Fuel of all kinds; all contrivances for, or means of, transportation on land, in the water or air, and machines used in their manufacture or repair; component parts thereof; instruments, articles, or animals necessary or convenient for their use; materials or ingredients used in their manufacture; articles necessary or convenient for the production or use of such materials or ingredients.
list.label: (c)
list.item: All means of communication, tools, implements, instruments, equipment, maps, pictures, papers and other articles, machines, or documents necessary or convenient for carrying on hostile operations; articles necessary or convenient for their manufacture or use.
list.label: (d)
list.item: Coin, bullion, currency, evidences of debt; also metal, materials, dies, plates, machinery, or other articles necessary or convenient for their manufacture.

div.p: (e) All kinds of food, foodstuffs, feed, forage, and clothing and articles and materials used in their production.
